title: Create a new liquidity pool
description: Create a new liquidity pool by converting an existing user issued asset into a pool share asset. Liquidity pools can swap any two assets on the blockchain with your own fee configuration.
taker_fee_content: Percentage charged to takers when they trade against your pool. Enter a value between 0 and 100.
withdrawal_fee_content: Percentage charged when liquidity is withdrawn from the pool. Enter a value between 0 and 100.
noEligibleAssets: No eligible assets available to create a liquidity pool with, it must be an user issued asset with no issued supply.
assetA_description: This is the first asset in the trading pair for the liquidity pool.
assetB_description: This is the second asset in the trading pair for the liquidity pool.
summary_ready: Your liquidity pool is ready to be created. Review the details below before submitting.
step1_description: Choose a user issued asset with no issued supply. It will be converted into this pool's share asset.
step2_description: These two assets will be traded against each other through your liquidity pool.
step3_description: Set the taker fee (charged on each trade) and the withdrawal fee (charged when liquidity leaves the pool). Values between 0 and 100%.
step1_max_supply_note: This asset becomes the pool's share asset. Make sure its maximum supply is large enough to represent all pool shares — an insufficient maximum can cause the pool to behave unexpectedly.
duplicate_assets: Both trading assets must be different. Please choose a distinct asset for each side of the pair.
